headline: DOWNGRADE proposal — first agent-baseline. Cumulative ECtHR Article 10 violation tally (432 since 2002, surfaced April 29 2026 by Stockholm CF and Turkish Minute) plus April 14-29 in-window pattern (5 named journalists sentenced, 1 arrested, EFJ April 28 condemnation of Article 217/A) confirms structural ACC/INT degradation that the published 32.8 under-reflects. Proposed composite 22.5 / Developing aligns with anchor-level evidence.
key_evidence: ECtHR cumulative 432 violations of Article 10 (freedom of expression) by Turkey since 2002 — published April 29 2026
April 14-15 2026: 5 named journalists sentenced (Soykan, Pehlivan, Ağırel, Boltan, Arapkirli) on disinformation/insulting-president charges
April 18-19 2026: Mehmet Yetim arrested under Article 217/A 'disinformation law'
April 28 2026: EFJ joint statement condemning escalating use of Article 217/A and calling for repeal
21 journalists imprisoned end of 2025 solely for journalism (IFJ)
Article 299 'insulting the president' prosecutions persist despite 2021 ECtHR ruling finding it incompatible with freedom of expression

evidence.finding: ECtHR cumulative 432 violations of Article 10 by Turkey since 2002. Pattern of non-compliance with binding ECtHR judgments documented.
Confirms 432 cumulative tally; structural rule-of-law erosion documented.
Joint statement condemning escalating use of disinformation law; 83 journalists charged 114 times since law entered force October 2022.
Government control over domestic courts and persistent ECtHR non-compliance documented.

negativeSignals: Cumulative ECtHR pattern (432 violations) is the diagnostic feature. April 14-29 in-window evidence — 5 sentenced journalists, 1 arrested, EFJ joint condemnation — confirms continuation rather than reform. Article 217/A application has expanded since October 2022 entry into force; 83 journalists / 114 charges accumulated.
positiveSignals: Continued refugee hosting (~3M Syrians); basic universal services functional; some ECtHR judgment compensation paid in individual cases.
